intent_description: Three-panel layout for a claim processing coder. Left panel: navigation. Middle panel: AI chat window for coding assistance. Right panel: worklist table showing pending claims with columns for patient name, payer, amount, issues, age (days), and status. Users can review and action claims from the worklist.

Building a claim processing coder worklist application with a three-panel layout: left navigation panel, center chat/assistant window, and right worklist panel. The worklist displays pending claims with columns for: claim ID, amount, patient name, payer, issues/denial reasons, age (days in queue), and status. The application is for medical billing coders and revenue cycle management staff who need to review, prioritize, and action insurance claims. Key UI elements needed: column headers with sort controls, status badges (e.g., Pending, In Review, Denied, Appealed, Approved), priority indicators (high/medium/low urgency), filtering controls by payer/status/age/amount, and row-level actions. Professional healthcare revenue cycle management context — clean, dense data layout suitable for power users who manage large claim volumes daily.

A three-panel claim processing worklist for a medical billing coder. Left navigation panel with queue links and tool links. Middle AI chat assistant panel for coding help (modifier lookup, denial reasons, code suggestions). Right main panel with a filterable, sortable table showing pending claims with columns: claim number, patient name/DOB, payer, dollar amount, issues/flags, age in days, and status badge. Status badges include Pending Coding, Pending Review, Needs Info, Denied. Issue flags highlight problems like missing modifiers, duplicate CPT, bundling conflicts, ICD mismatch. Stats strip shows totals and aging alerts above the table.
Three-panel full-page layout for a claim processing coder tool: left navigation panel, center chat window, and right worklist panel. Fixed height viewport layout with persistent panels.

Left navigation panel for a claim processing coder. Shows nav links such as Worklist, Claims, Patients, Payers, Reports, and Settings. Includes user avatar/profile at the bottom and an app logo/title at the top.

Chat panel for a claim processing coder tool. Shows a conversation history between the coder and an AI assistant about claim coding questions. Includes a message input with send button at the bottom. Messages are labeled by sender with timestamps.

Worklist table for a claim processing coder. Displays pending claims with columns: Claim ID, Patient (Last, First), Payer, Amount, Issues (count/description), Age (days since submission), and Status badge. Supports row selection and row hover. Status can be: Pending Review, In Progress, Needs Info, Approved, Denied.

Status badge pill for claim status states: Pending Review (open/neutral), In Progress (primary/blue), Needs Info (warning/yellow), Approved (success/green), Denied (destructive/red). Rounded-full pill shape with label text. Color must not be the sole differentiator.
user clicks a button to submit a form
user clicks a button to submit a form

Left sidebar navigation for a medical coder SaaS application. Contains an app logo/title at top, a Home navigation item, and a scrollable list of past claim threads auto-named by patient name and claim ID. Active thread is highlighted. Used by medical coders to navigate between claim review sessions.

AI chat interface panel for medical coders. Center column of a 3-column layout. Shows chat message history (user and assistant messages), contextual nudge prompt chips/buttons above the input that change based on whether the user is viewing the claims worklist or a specific claim detail. Has a text input with send button at bottom. Always visible regardless of right panel content.

Claim detail panel for medical coders. Right column view shown when a claim row is clicked. Shows expanded claim information: all worklist fields plus full diagnosis codes (ICD-10), procedure codes (CPT), provider name, facility, date of service. Includes a notes and audit trail section at the bottom. Professional SaaS layout with section groupings.

Full 3-column page layout for a medical coder claims management SaaS application. Left column is a fixed sidebar with navigation and thread history. Center column is an AI chat panel. Right column is the main content area showing either a claims worklist table or a claim detail view. Professional medical SaaS aesthetic, high information density.

Left sidebar navigation for a medical coder SaaS app. Contains app logo/title at top, a Home navigation item to return to worklist, and a scrollable list of past claim threads auto-named by patient name and claim ID. Active thread is highlighted. Professional, compact design.

AI chat interface for medical coders. Center column always visible. Shows contextual quick-action chips/nudge buttons above the input that change based on whether worklist view or claim detail view is active. Full message history per thread with user and AI messages. Clean chat input with send button at bottom. Professional clinical SaaS aesthetic.

Claim detail panel shown in the right column when a claim row is clicked. Displays all worklist fields plus: full diagnosis codes (ICD-10), procedure codes (CPT), provider name, facility, date of service, and a notes/audit trail section. Expandable, high information density layout. Professional medical records view.

Full 3-column page layout for a medical coder claims worklist application. Left sidebar (240px) for navigation and thread history, center column (fixed ~380px) for persistent AI chat, right column (flex-1) for worklist or claim detail. Professional SaaS shell with fixed height viewport layout.
